assembly_instructions: Open-source 1U CubeSat (10×10×10 cm) from Portland State Aerospace Society. Four 6061-aluminum corner rails with machined +Y and −Y end-caps form the primary structure. Two body-mounted solar panels flush-cover the +Z and −Z side faces; the +X and −X sides carry deployable solar panels hinged on burn-wire releases at the top of each rail (shown deployed 90° outward). Inside the open cage, a PC/104 stack of four cards (battery, ADACS, on-board computer, communications) plugs into a vertical backplane PCB along the −Z interior wall. Exterior sensors: a small camera lens in the +Z face, a sun-sensor chip and a magnetometer on the top end-cap, a UHF whip antenna deployed straight up from the top end-cap, and a deployment-switch plunger on a bottom corner rail (arms the satellite when the P-POD dispenser releases it).

info.summary: Open-hardware 1U CubeSat from the Portland State Aerospace Society — a technology-demonstration precursor to the larger OreSat bus.
info.description: OreSat0 is an open-source 1U (10×10×10 cm) CubeSat developed by the Portland State Aerospace Society (PSAS). It was built to flight-qualify the structural, power, and command-and-data-handling systems of the larger OreSat bus before those subsystems flew on a 2U/3U science mission. Four 6061 aluminum corner rails carry the launch loads, closed off by machined +Y and -Y end-caps. Inside, a PC/104-form-factor stack holds a battery card, an ADACS (Attitude Determination and Control) module, an on-board computer, and a UHF comms card — all plugging into a shared backplane that runs the power and CAN bus between them. Two deployable solar panels hinge outward from opposite side rails on burn-wire releases; the other two side faces carry flush body-mounted solar cells. External features: a CMOS camera lens in one side face, a sun sensor and magnetometer on the top face, a UHF whip antenna, and a deployment switch on one bottom corner that arms the satellite on release from the P-POD dispenser. Structure and electronics are released under CERN-OHL-S v2.0.
info.contractor: PSAS student/volunteer team, Portland State University
info.status: Flown (deorbited). Design iterated into OreSat (2U+) flight bus.

facts.label: Avionics bus
facts.value: PC/104 stack with shared backplane, CAN-based

facts.label: Power
facts.value: Body-mounted solar (+Z, −Z) + two deployable side panels → Li-ion battery card

parts.name: Corner Rail (Fwd-Right, +X+Z)
parts.role: Machined 6061 corner rail — one of four carrying launch-rail loads.

parts.name: Corner Rail (Fwd-Left, +X−Z)
parts.role: Forward-left corner rail.

parts.name: Corner Rail (Rear-Right, −X+Z)
parts.role: Rear-right corner rail.

parts.name: Corner Rail (Rear-Left, −X−Z)
parts.role: Rear-left corner rail. Houses the deployment switch on the −Y end.

parts.name: End Cap (+Y, top)
parts.role: Machined top end-cap. Mounts the sun sensor, magnetometer, and UHF antenna.

parts.name: End Cap (−Y, bottom)
parts.role: Bottom end-cap. Interfaces to the P-POD dispenser's launch-rail pusher spring.

parts.name: Body-Mounted Solar Panel (+Z)
parts.role: Body-mounted solar cells flush against the +Z face.

parts.name: Body-Mounted Solar Panel (−Z)
parts.role: Body-mounted solar cells flush against the −Z face.

parts.name: Deployable Solar Panel (+X)
parts.role: Deployable solar panel hinged on the +X rail; shown flipped outward 90°.

parts.name: Deployable Solar Panel (−X)
parts.role: Deployable solar panel hinged on the −X rail.

parts.name: Burn-Wire Hinge (+X)
parts.role: Spring-loaded hinge pin on the +X rail — released by a burn-wire at deployment.

parts.name: Burn-Wire Hinge (−X)
parts.role: Burn-wire hinge on the −X rail.

parts.name: PC/104 Backplane
parts.role: Vertical backplane PCB along the interior −Z wall — carries power + CAN bus between the stacked PC/104 cards.

parts.name: Battery Card (PC/104)
parts.role: Li-ion battery board — bottom of the stack.

parts.name: ADACS Card (PC/104)
parts.role: Attitude Determination and Control board — IMU + magnetometer + torque-rod drivers.

parts.name: On-Board Computer Card
parts.role: Mission computer running the C3 flight software.

parts.name: UHF Comms Card (PC/104)
parts.role: UHF radio board — drives the whip antenna.

parts.name: CMOS Camera Lens
parts.role: Small lens protruding through the +Z body-mounted panel — the tech-demo imaging camera.

parts.name: Sun Sensor
parts.role: Photodiode-based coarse sun sensor on the top end-cap. Feeds ADACS.

parts.name: Magnetometer
parts.role: 3-axis magnetometer chip on the top end-cap for coarse attitude sensing.

parts.name: UHF Whip Antenna
parts.role: Deployed monopole UHF whip — unfurls from its base on the top end-cap after orbit insertion.

parts.name: Deployment Switch
parts.role: Plunger switch on one bottom corner rail — held in by the P-POD dispenser and released at deployment to arm the satellite.
